page: UpCloud 2xCPU-4GB is a general-purpose virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 shared vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7542 processor operating at 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, and includes 80 GB of local SSD storage. Network connectivity includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data indicates that this server is positioned at the lower end of the performance spectrum. In stress-ng testing, both single-core and multi-core CPU performance fall into the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with large-block read and write speeds of 14.52 GB/sec and 10.94 GB/sec respectively, placing them in the bottom 10%. However, single-threaded operations show moderate capability, with gzip compression and bzip3 decompression achieving average performance tiers. Geekbench testing yields a single-core score of 1166.0 (average) and a multi-core score of 2017.0 (weak).

Given the shared CPU allocation and modest memory capacity, this server type represents a budget-friendly option for workloads with low resource demands. It is not suitable for memory-intensive databases, heavy multi-threaded compilation, or high-throughput web serving, as demonstrated by its low Redis and static web serving benchmark results. The instance is best suited for lightweight applications, basic development environments, small-scale single-threaded tasks, and low-traffic utility services.
description: UpCloud 2xCPU-4GB is a general-purpose virtual server utilizing the KVM hypervisor. It is configured with 2 shared vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 80 GB of local SSD storage. Performance benchmarks indicate that this instance is suited for low-intensity workloads, with CPU and memory bandwidth tests generally placing in the lower percentiles, though single-threaded tasks like gzip compression perform at an average level. The shared CPU allocation makes this a cost-effective choice for non-production environments. It is best suited for lightweight utility services, basic development tasks, and low-traffic web hosting.
